editor.failedNbtLoad: Failed to load world NBT data.
editor.worldIdentityDescription: Rename the world here or change the world icon.
editor.worldTitleHint: This is the name players see inside Minecraft.
editor.worldIdHint: This is the folder name on disk.
editor.iconMustBePng: World icon must be a PNG file.
editor.iconWillReplace: World icon will be replaced when you save.
editor.worldIconHint: Select a PNG to replace icon.png for the Minecraft world list.
editor.simpleModeLead: Simple mode groups the common edits by category, adds beginner-friendly help text, and lets every saved player keep their own dropdown for separate editing.
editor.advancedModeLead: Advanced mode edits the full world data view as JSON for the world and the primary player. The other player dropdowns keep their own edits when you switch tabs.
editor.hotbarPickNote: Choose the hotbar slot the player should have selected when the world loads.
editor.inventoryLayoutDescription: Use the slot picker if you want a visual hotbar, inventory, armor, and offhand layout, or type a different slot number manually when you need something more advanced.
editor.enderChestDescription: This is the personal storage this player can open from any ender chest, and the picker shows the same 9x3 layout Minecraft uses.
editor.slotPickerNote: This picker covers inventory, hotbar, armor, and offhand slots (if compatible).

tooltips.gameMode:
Changes how the world plays for the current player.

Survival: Normal gameplay.
Creative: Unlimited blocks and flying.
Adventure: Restricted map play.
Spectator: Free-fly camera mode.
tooltips.difficulty:
Changes how harsh the world feels.

Higher difficulty means tougher enemies and faster hunger loss.
tooltips.allowCommands:
Turns commands on or off for this world.

Useful if you want things like teleporting, giving items, or using other cheats.
tooltips.hardcore:
Marks the world as Hardcore.

Hardcore worlds are much less forgiving and are meant for a one-life style challenge.
tooltips.spawnX: The default X coordinate where players respawn in this world.
tooltips.spawnY: The default Y height where players respawn in this world.
tooltips.spawnZ: The default Z coordinate where players respawn in this world.
tooltips.timeOfDay:
Controls where the sun or moon is right now.

Common values:
0: Sunrise
6000: Midday
12000: Sunset
18000: Midnight
tooltips.rainDuration: How long rain should stay active before Minecraft can clear it again.
tooltips.thunderDuration: How long the thunderstorm should stay active before Minecraft can calm it down.
tooltips.health:
The player's current health.

20 is full health for a normal player.
tooltips.foodLevel:
The player's current hunger bar.

20 is full hunger.
tooltips.foodSaturation: How long the hunger bar stays full before it starts dropping.
tooltips.xpLevel: The green experience level number shown above the hotbar.
tooltips.xpTotal: The player's total saved experience points.
tooltips.playerX:
The player's saved X coordinate.

This decides where they will stand when the world loads.
tooltips.playerY:
The player's saved Y height.

This decides how high or low they will be when the world loads.
tooltips.playerZ:
The player's saved Z coordinate.

This decides where they will stand when the world loads.
tooltips.inventorySlot:
The slot number used for this item.

Use the picker button if you want a visual hotbar, inventory, armor, and offhand layout instead of remembering slot numbers.
tooltips.inventoryItem:
The item id to place in this slot.

Example: minecraft:stone
tooltips.inventoryCount: How many items should be in this slot.
tooltips.enderSlot:
The ender chest slot number.

Use the picker button if you want a visual 9x3 ender chest layout.
tooltips.enderItem:
The item id to place in this ender chest slot.

Example: minecraft:diamond_block
tooltips.enderCount: How many items should be in this ender chest slot.
tooltips.hotbarSlot:
Which hotbar slot is selected when the player loads in.

This is shown as 1 through 9 to match what players normally see in game.

player.label: Player
player.willCreateData: Player data will be created when you save
player.noSavedDataYet: {player} does not have saved player data in this world yet. Any fields you set here will be created when you save.
player.statsDescription: Health, hunger, experience, and the selected hotbar slot are all saved per player.
player.hotbarHint: Shown as 1 through 9, just like the in-game hotbar.
player.positionDescription: This is where this player appears when they join the world.

Histolauncher: Personal account, your own custom skin customizeable from the website.

Microsoft: Official Minecraft account login for online play.

microsoft.useCodeInstruction: Use this code to sign in with your Microsoft account:

account.playerPreviewTooltip: Show the player preview with angled body and cape rendering when account textures are available.

appearance.uiSizeTooltip: Scale launcher text, controls, icons, and navigation.
appearance.languageTooltip: Choose the launcher display language. Community translations live in external locale files.
appearance.densityTooltip: Choose how much spacing the launcher uses.
appearance.compactSidebarTooltip: Collapse the sidebar to icons only.

client.minRamTooltip:
Minimum RAM allocated to the game
  - Must be a positive number
  - Must be smaller than Maximum RAM

Example format: 256, 256M, 2G, etc.

(performance tip: allocate more RAM for better performance, also match it with maximum RAM for less frame drops)
client.ramInvalidTitle: RAM format: number with optional K, M, G, or T suffix (e.g., 2048M, 2G)
client.maxRamTooltip:
Maximum RAM allocated to the game
  - Must be a positive number
  - Must be greater than Minimum RAM
  - Must be at least 1 byte

Example format: 256, 256M, 2G, etc.

(performance tip: allocate more RAM for better performance, also match it with minimum RAM for less frame drops)
client.resolutionTooltip: Default game window size used for versions that support width and height launch arguments.
client.fullscreenTooltip: Launch supported versions in fullscreen by default.
client.demoModeTooltip: Launch supported versions with Minecraft Demo Mode enabled.
client.storageDirTooltip:
Global: All game versions share the same data directory.

Version: Separate game data directory for each version, best used for testing or version sandboxing. (deleting the version also deletes the data)

Custom: Use a folder you choose yourself. The folder must exist before launch.
client.storageInvalidTitle: Select a valid custom storage folder before launching.
client.failedSelectCustomStorageDirectory: Failed to select a custom storage directory.
client.failedSelectCustomStorageDirectoryWithEr:
Failed to select a custom storage directory.

{error}
client.extraJvmTooltip:
Extra JVM arguments appended to every launch command. Useful for performance tuning or debugging.

Example: -XX:+UseG1GC -XX:MaxGCPauseMillis=50

launcher.advancedWarning: WARNING: These are advanced settings that can change the launcher's behavior in unexpected ways!
launcher.javaRuntimeTooltip:
Choose which Java runtime to use for Minecraft launches.

Auto inspects the version, tries the matching installed Java first, then climbs through detected Java versions until one works.

Default (Java PATH) always uses the Java command available on your system PATH.
launcher.urlProxyTooltip: Routes all launcher requests through a proxy server. (having an invalid URL will cause requests to fail, recommended to keep empty)
launcher.lowDataTooltip: Downloads ONLY the required client & libraries (no sounds, no extra textures, recommended for slow connections and/or low storage)
launcher.fastDownloadTooltip: Uses aggressive parallel downloads (may cause issues, not recommended unless needed)
launcher.thirdPartyVersionsTooltip: Show additional community-provided versions from Omniarchive in the Versions list.
launcher.discordStatusTooltip: Show Discord Rich Presence while the launcher or game is running.

mods.overwriteClasspath: Allow overwrite classpath for all modloaders:
mods.overwriteClasspathTooltip:
When enabled, the mod-level 'Overwrite classes' option can be used for all supported mod loaders.

When disabled, overwrite classes remains limited to legacy ModLoader behavior only.

dataStorage.logsTooltip: Clears all log files of both clients and the launcher (helps free up disk space)
dataStorage.diagnosticsTooltip: Builds a redacted launcher diagnostics report with system, Java, version, add-on, and recent log metadata for troubleshooting.

about.intro: Histolauncher is a lightweight, community-driven launcher built to grow and help users play any versions of Minecraft they like. It provides a clean, modern interface for browsing and launching a massive library of Minecraft clients, all officially from Mojang!
about.goal: The goal is simple: make it easy for anyone to play any Minecraft version offline without having to risk searching for 100% clean launcher. Everything is organized, searchable, and ready to download & launch with just a few clicks.
about.contribute: If you'd like to contribute, report issues, or help expand support for more versions, you're invited to join the project on GitHub:
about.outro: Histolauncher is a passion project - built for easy access to Minecraft clients with offline-mode support. Enjoy playing Minecraft!

browserFallback.title: Histolauncher - Control Panel for Browser Users
browserFallback.description:
This is the control panel for browser users.

Click "Open Launcher" to open the launcher's web link in your default browser.
Click "Close Launcher" to close the web server and exit Histolauncher.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.noteFresh: NOTE: No Histolauncher data folder has been created yet, so you can safely delete the launcher files if you choose not to proceed.
disclaimer.noteExisting: NOTE: Histolauncher will not continue until you accept. Existing local data remains untouched.
disclaimer.message:
DISCLAIMER: Histolauncher is a third-party Minecraft launcher and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or associated with Mojang Studios or Microsoft.

All Minecraft game files are downloaded directly from Mojang's official servers. Histolauncher does not host, modify, or redistribute any proprietary Minecraft files.

By selecting OK, you acknowledge that you have read and agreed to the Minecraft EULA (https://www.minecraft.net/en-us/eula) and understand that Histolauncher is an independent project with no official connection to Mojang or Microsoft.

If you do NOT agree, please press "Cancel" and do not use this launcher.

{note}

prompts.betaWarningMessage:
This is a beta version of Histolauncher. You may encounter bugs during use, so please keep that in mind. If you encounter any problems or bugs, please report them on GitHub or Discord as soon as possible.

(beta version: {local})
prompts.createShortcutMessage:
Would you like Histolauncher to create a shortcut now?

This adds Histolauncher to your Start Menu on Windows or your applications menu on Linux, and it can be repaired later with shortcut.pyw or shortcut.sh.
prompts.newUserMessage:
Hi there, new user! Welcome to Histolauncher!

Would you like to read INSTRUCTIONS.txt for more information about this launcher and how to enable special features such as debug mode?
prompts.shortcutErrorMessage: Histolauncher could not create or repair the shortcut.
prompts.updateAvailableMessage:
Histolauncher is out of date!

Would you like to automatically download the latest version now? Be aware that this will delete everything inside the launcher directory and reinstall everything fresh from the Histolauncher GitHub repository.

(your version: {local}, latest version: {remote})
prompts.updateInstalledMessage: Histolauncher has been updated and will now restart.
prompts.updateFailedMessage: The update failed and Histolauncher attempted to restore from backup. Check logs for details.

updater.heading: Updating Histolauncher
updater.clearBeforeRestoreFailed: Could not clear partial update before restore: {error}
updater.backupMissing: Backup file was not found in %temp%.

languagePrompt.title: Choose your language
languagePrompt.message: Choose the language to use for the disclaimer and startup dialogs. This choice is temporary until you accept the disclaimer.
